I will run only these path-scoped, test-only restore commands (and I have not run them yet):

```bash
# 1) Restore deleted test files under bridge/gilt-exec only
git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain diff --name-only --diff-filter=D -- bridge/gilt-exec \
| rg '(^|/)(tests?|testdata)(/|$)|_test\.' \
| xargs -r git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain restore --

# 2) Restore deleted test files under bridge/gilt-consensus only
git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain diff --name-only --diff-filter=D -- bridge/gilt-consensus \
| rg '(^|/)(tests?|testdata)(/|$)|_test\.' \
| xargs -r git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain restore --

# 3) Restore deleted test files under gilt-chain only
git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain diff --name-only --diff-filter=D -- gilt-chain \
| rg '(^|/)(tests?|testdata)(/|$)|_test\.' \
| xargs -r git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain restore --

# 4) Verify no deleted test files remain in those three paths
git -C /srv/repos/gold-chain diff --name-only --diff-filter=D -- bridge/gilt-exec bridge/gilt-consensus gilt-chain \
| rg '(^|/)(tests?|testdata)(/|$)|_test\.'
```

This matters because it restores only test-suite paths and leaves non-test files untouched.

Next: if you confirm, I will run exactly these commands and then report only the resulting test-file status.
